changes.value:
The indexing worker in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs` (line 72) opens its own SQLite connection without setting `busy_timeout`. When the LSP worker holds a write lock, the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` immediately and silently fails to mark files as indexed. Those files stay `ts_indexed=0` forever, causing the progress to stall at ~91%.

**Fix**: Add `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;` to the connection setup at line 73 of `indexing.rs`. This makes SQLite retry for up to 5 seconds instead of failing immediately on write contention.

**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`

#bug #code-context
fix: add PRAGMA busy_timeout to indexing worker DB connection

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,7 +1,9 @@
-The indexing worker in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs` (line 72) opens its own SQLite connection without setting `busy_timeout`. When the LSP worker holds a write lock, the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` immediately and silently fails to mark files as indexed. Those files stay `ts_indexed=0` forever, causing the progress to stall at ~91%.
+The indexing worker in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs` opens its own `Connection::open(db_path)` (line 72). This creates write contention with the LSP worker, which uses the leader's `SharedDb` (`Arc<Mutex<Connection>>`). SQLite WAL allows only one writer at a time across connections — the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` and silently fails to mark files indexed, causing progress to stall at ~91%.
+
+**Root cause**: Multiple DB connections instead of a single write queue.

-**Fix**: Add `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;` to the connection setup at line 73 of `indexing.rs`. This makes SQLite retry for up to 5 seconds instead of failing immediately on write contention.
+**Fix**: Change `spawn_indexing_worker` to accept a `SharedDb` instead of a `db_path`. All writes (chunk inserts, index marks) go through the shared mutex, same as the LSP worker. This eliminates cross-connection contention entirely — no need for `busy_timeout` hacks.

-**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`
+**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lib.rs` (or wherever the worker is spawned)

SQLite WAL allows only one writer at a time across connections — the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` and silently fails to mark files indexed, causing progress to stall at ~91%.
+The indexing worker in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs` (line 72) opens its own SQLite connection without setting `busy_timeout`. When the LSP worker holds a write lock, the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` immediately and silently fails to mark files as indexed. Those files stay `ts_indexed=0` forever, causing the progress to stall at ~91%.

-**Root cause**: Multiple DB connections instead of a single write queue.

-**Fix**: Change `spawn_indexing_worker` to accept a `SharedDb` instead of a `db_path`. All writes (chunk inserts, index marks) go through the shared mutex, same as the LSP worker. This eliminates cross-connection contention entirely — no need for `busy_timeout` hacks.

-**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lib.rs` (or wherever the worker is spawned)

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,9 +1,15 @@
-The indexing worker in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs` opens its own `Connection::open(db_path)` (line 72). This creates write contention with the LSP worker, which uses the leader's `SharedDb` (`Arc<Mutex<Connection>>`). SQLite WAL allows only one writer at a time across connections — the indexing worker gets `SQLITE_BUSY` and silently fails to mark files indexed, causing progress to stall at ~91%.
+The indexing worker opens its own `Connection::open(db_path)` (line 72 of `indexing.rs`). The LSP worker uses the leader's `SharedDb`. Two connections = write contention under WAL = `SQLITE_BUSY` = files stuck at `ts_indexed=0` = 91% stall.
+
+**Root cause**: No centralized write queue. Multiple workers open independent connections and race for SQLite's single-writer lock.

-**Root cause**: Multiple DB connections instead of a single write queue.
+**Fix**: Introduce a DB write queue owned by the leader. All writers (indexing worker, LSP worker, cleanup) submit write operations to the queue rather than holding their own connections. The leader election already determines who writes — the write queue makes that guarantee structural rather than implicit.
+
+Design options:
+- `mpsc::channel` of write operations processed by a dedicated writer thread
+- Or a `SharedDb` wrapper that serializes all writes through a single connection + mutex

-**Fix**: Change `spawn_indexing_worker` to accept a `SharedDb` instead of a `db_path`. All writes (chunk inserts, index marks) go through the shared mutex, same as the LSP worker. This eliminates cross-connection contention entirely — no need for `busy_timeout` hacks.
+Either way: one connection, one writer, no contention. Workers become pure producers of write intents.

-**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lib.rs` (or wherever the worker is spawned)
+**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/indexing.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lsp_worker.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lib.rs`

This creates write contention with the LSP worker, which uses the leader's `SharedDb` (`Arc<Mutex<Connection>>`). -**Fix**: Introduce a DB write queue owned by the leader. All writers (indexing worker, LSP worker, cleanup) submit write operations to the queue rather than holding their own connections.
